body.value: 鹿児島藩 — 書ナラス（かきならず）＝「記載なし／書面に出さない」

Kagoshima (Satsuma) domain, Table 8. Its ENTIRE military disclosure is the two-character note 書ナラス ('not written / not specified') — a near-total refusal. Combined with Satsuma's actual ~16,000-Spencer-rifle force, this made it the most opaque military presence in the 1870 survey. Everything to the RIGHT of this ○ marker (read first, right-to-left) is the tail of the PREVIOUS domain, Kanazawa.
兵卒人員 千五百六十八人 ／ 隊士 四百六十四人 ／ 士等役員 千百六人 ／（参事 一・九・二十一・十六）
これは前の藩（金澤藩）のデータ — 兵卒人員1,568・隊士464・士等役員1,106・参事計47

NOT Satsuma's. These figures — 兵卒人員 1,568 (rank-and-file), 隊士 464 (corps members), 士等役員 1,106 (officer-rank), plus the four councillor counts (1 + 9 + 21 + 16 = 47) — sit to the RIGHT of the ○鹿兒島藩 marker, so in right-to-left reading order they belong to the PRECEDING domain 金澤 (Kanazawa), whose entry spills onto this page from the prior page. MASTER re-tagged them 鹿児島藩→金澤藩 on 2026-05-28; they are no longer attributed to Satsuma.
高知藩（こうちはん）＝土佐藩 — 次の藩の始まり

Kōchi (Tosa) domain — the NEXT entry begins here, to the LEFT of Satsuma's marker. Unlike Satsuma, Tosa discloses a full named officer roster below (知事・大参事・権大参事… 深尾弘人, 五藤如山, 齋藤彌九馬…). Its presence confirms that the only content between the two ○ markers — Satsuma's — is 書ナラス.
